turns.text: Netflix added 7.3 million subscribers in Q3 to reach 316 million, on track for our year-end target. Advertising revenue crossed $1 billion in quarterly revenue for the first time. We announced a major expansion of our live sports portfolio: a 10-year deal for Formula 1 global streaming rights beginning in 2026, valued at approximately $1.5 billion. We expect this to be the most significant subscriber acquisition catalyst since the password-sharing crackdown.

Revenue was $11.6 billion, up 15% year-over-year. Operating margin was 32.1%. We are now guiding full-year operating margin to 31–32%, above our prior guidance of 29–30%, reflecting advertising upside. For Q4, we expect revenue of $12.1–12.5 billion and operating margin of approximately 22%, reflecting typical seasonality in content costs.
Our next question comes from Mark Mahaney at Evercore ISI.
Greg, Formula 1 at $1.5 billion over 10 years — how does that pencil out on a subscriber acquisition cost basis relative to your historical content ROI framework?

statements.claim: Netflix advertising revenue crossed $1 billion in quarterly revenue for the first time in Q3 2025.
Netflix signed a 10-year Formula 1 global streaming rights deal beginning in 2026, valued at approximately $1.5 billion.
Netflix is raising full-year 2025 operating margin guidance to 31–32%, above the prior 29–30% guidance.
The Formula 1 deal will be the most significant subscriber acquisition catalyst since the password-sharing crackdown.
Netflix reached 316 million subscribers at end of Q3 2025.
